original: Wakaishu ya / shinu ga iya nara / ima shiniyare
english: If death frightens you, die before death arrives.
spanish: Si la muerte te asusta, muere antes de que llegue la muerte.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, frontispiece / Hakuin
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, frontispiece. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

original: Haiku: one state or event in the present.
english: A death poem can leave one image instead of an explanation.
spanish: Un poema de muerte puede dejar una imagen en vez de una explicación.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, Introduction
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, pp. 22-24. Conceptual jisei artifact, not a copied poem.

original: Kura yakete / sawaru mono naki / tsukimi kana
english: When the storehouse burns, the moon has no obstruction.
spanish: Cuando arde el granero, la luna queda sin obstáculo.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 23. Short working rendering.

original: Umi kurete / kamo no koe / honoka ni shiroshi
english: At the darkening sea, sound and whiteness meet for an instant.
spanish: En el mar que oscurece, sonido y blancura se encuentran por un instante.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 25. Short working rendering.

original: Haru nare ya / na mo naki yama no / usugasumi
english: Spring arrives; even the nameless mountain receives mist.
spanish: Llega la primavera; hasta la montaña sin nombre recibe niebla.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 25. Short working rendering.

original: Yamadori no / o o fumu haru no / irihi kana
english: The spring sun goes down as if stepping on a pheasant's tail.
spanish: El sol de primavera cae como si pisara la cola de un faisán.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, pp. 25-26. Short working rendering.

original: Akikaze ya / hyorohyoro yama no / kageboshi
english: Autumn wind: the mountain's shadow trembles like a body.
spanish: Viento de otoño: la sombra de la montaña tiembla como un cuerpo.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 26. Short working rendering.

original: Mizu no tsuki / mondori utte / nagarekeri
english: The moon in water turns over and flows away.
spanish: La luna en el agua gira y se va con la corriente.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 26. Short working rendering.

original: Mugi-guruma / uma ni okurete / ugoki izu
english: The wagon lags, then leaps behind the horse.
spanish: El carro se retrasa, luego salta detrás del caballo.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 26. Short working rendering.

original: Aki no kure / hi ya tomosan to / toi ni kuru
english: Autumn evening: someone asks if it is time to light the lantern.
spanish: Tarde de otoño: alguien pregunta si ya es hora de encender la lámpara.
recommendedEditionSource: Yoel Hoffmann — Japanese Death Poems, haiku discussion
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Hoffmann PDF, p. 27. Short working rendering.
